text: Debugging is an essential skill for any programmer. When your code doesn’t work as expected, the first step is identifying the bug by reproducing the problem. A systematic approach helps you locate errors efficiently: break the code into small parts and test each one. Debugging tools like breakpoints allow you to pause execution and inspect variables. Logging is also effective—add print statements or use a logging library to track what happens during runtime. By combining these strategies, you can fix mistakes faster and learn from them.
